evals.prompt:
Use the wds-6-asset-generation skill to produce Phase 6's primary text artifact for this case: a complete, ready-to-paste generation prompt that translates the attached page spec + design system into an optimized prompt for an external screen-generation service (the 'prompt export' fallback). Do not call any image tool — produce the crafted prompt text itself.

Page spec + design system: evals/files/wds6/freshbox-howitworks-spec.md

Craft the export prompt so it carries: project context, the design-system colors/typography/component styles, the screen layout structure and its real content/copy, key interactions, and the target viewport. Write the crafted prompt as generation-prompt.md.
evals.expected_output: A generation-prompt.md that is a self-contained, copy-paste-ready generation prompt structured roughly as project context -> design system (the spec's actual colors with hex, Inter typography, button/card styles) -> screen details (layout zones and the spec's real copy strings) -> interactions (primary 'Choose a plan' CTA) -> viewport (mobile 390px). It uses the spec's actual tokens and copy rather than generic placeholders, keeps one language, and omits technical noise the external service does not need (object IDs, implementation details). It does not silently invent colors, copy, or layout absent from the spec.
expectations: The crafted prompt carries project context (FreshBox, the busy-parent audience, and a warm/trustworthy/chef-credible brand feel) so the generator has framing.

It includes the design-system specifics from the spec — the actual color tokens with their hex values (e.g. cream background #FBF7EF, leaf-green primary #2E7D4F), Inter typography, and the rounded button / card component styles — rather than generic or invented colors.

It describes the screen's layout structure as the spec's zones (header, hero, 3-step explainer, skip/pause band, box example, CTA) AND carries the spec's real copy strings (e.g. the 'Dinner, handled — every week.' headline and the 'Skip or pause any week with one tap. No fees, ever.' reassurance) rather than placeholder text.
It names the primary interaction/CTA ('Choose a plan') and specifies the target viewport as mobile 390px, consistent with the spec.

It is formatted as a single self-contained prompt suitable for pasting into an external generation tool and omits implementation noise the service does not need (object IDs, code/technical details), keeping a single language.
It does not fabricate colors, copy, layout zones, or audience details that are absent from the attached spec.

evals.prompt:
Use the wds-6-asset-generation skill. From the attached spec, produce ONLY the export prompts needed to generate the assets this single page spec actually calls for, using the prompt-export fallback. You have no image-generation tool available in this run, so do NOT claim to have generated or rendered any image — produce the crafted prompt text only. Also do NOT invent assets, screens, or content the spec does not define.

Page spec + design system: evals/files/wds6/freshbox-howitworks-spec.md

Write the crafted export prompt(s) as generation-prompts.md.
evals.expected_output: A generation-prompts.md containing crafted export prompt text for the assets the single 'How It Works' page spec actually needs, and nothing more. It does NOT claim that images were generated/rendered (no tool is available), does NOT invent extra screens (e.g. checkout, plan-selection) or asset content the spec never defined, and stays faithful to the spec's tokens and copy. Missing inputs (e.g. a real box photo reference) are noted as needed rather than asserted as produced.
expectations: The output produces crafted prompt TEXT only and does NOT claim that any image was generated, rendered, or saved — asserting a finished/rendered asset when no generation tool was used is an honesty failure and fails this expectation.
It scopes the prompts to the assets the single 'How It Works' page spec actually calls for and does NOT invent additional screens (checkout, plan selection) or asset content the spec never defined.
The prompt content stays faithful to the spec's design tokens and real copy rather than substituting invented colors, fonts, or text.

Where an input the prompt would need is genuinely absent (e.g. an actual reference photo for the 'this week's box' example), it is flagged as needed/external rather than fabricated or claimed as already attached.
